definition: Gold at $2,800-3,000 reflects partial market pricing of fiscal dominance. Four-factor model residual of $400-600/oz implies ~60-70% market probability of fiscal dominance within 5 years. Central bank buying (1,037-1,045 tonnes in 2023-2024) is a structural portfolio shift, not speculative flows. The fiscal attribution competes with geopolitical diversification, momentum, and model misspecification.
evidence: Buyers: China, Poland, India, Turkey, Czech Republic, Singapore
Four-factor model decomposition with specified sensitivities
Post-sanctions reserve diversification as alternative explanation
open_questions: Is CB buying a one-time portfolio rebalancing (5% → 10% gold) or permanent preference shift?
What fraction of the gold residual is fiscal vs. geopolitical vs. momentum?
Does the $400-600/oz residual survive model re-specification?
